title: Generating HTML class Attributes for Styling
breadcrumbs: Generating HTML class Attributes for Styling
text: Generating HTML class Attributes for Styling The HTML generated by Uniface for dynamic server page components includes HTML attributes for formatting the layout. These attributes are determined by declaratively-defined widget definitions, dynamically-set properties, and HTML attributes explicitly defined in the server page layout. HTML elements are more commonly styled using Cascading Style Sheets, which apply a set of properties to the element based on the value of its class attribute. The class attribute may specify multiple classes. Uniface automatically generates elements with custom class attributes for: Bound syntax errors. For more information, see Syntax Checking in Dynamic Server Pages . Empty entity and occurrence elements. For more information, see Styling Entities and Occurrences in HTML . Mobile app layouts defined by the data-uniface-role attribute. For more information, see Mobile App Layout and data-uniface-role . You can use Uniface's class attributes to apply your own styles. For example, you could suppress empty occurrences or display errors based on the class applied to the element. For example: <style media="screen" type="text/css"> .-uocc-empty- { display: none; } </style> Copy In addition to Uniface's own classes, you can instruct Uniface to add your own styles to any element bound to a Uniface object. To do so, you can set the class: ClassName widget property. For more information, see class:ClassName . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
